entries.position_summary: Supports legally binding negative security assurances for NNWS consistent with UNSC Resolution 984. Conditions include NNWS refraining from aggression against NWS or their allies and full compliance with nonproliferation obligations, including renouncing any control over nuclear weapons via alliance arrangements.
entries.quote: We support the non-nuclear-weapon States parties to the NPT in seeking legally binding negative security assurances (NSA), which would prevent use or threat of use of nuclear weapons against these States and which would be equivalent to those set forth in UNSC resolution 984. It is important that these States should neither engage in nor facilitate aggression against nuclear-weapon States and/or their allies and should comply with their nuclear non-proliferation obligations, which includes the renunciation from having nuclear weapons under control in the framework of military alliances with nuclear-weapons States.

entries.position_summary: The country calls for greater transparency and accountability from all states, with particular emphasis on NATO non-nuclear-weapon States involved in nuclear sharing. It urges these states to provide maximum, detailed reporting on their nuclear-related infrastructure, its use, and future plans.
entries.quote: Going back to the matters of transparency and accountability, we believe that all countries should contribute to this process. At the same time, there is a number of States that are considered non-nuclear-weapon States under the NPT, yet they are in fact NATO members, they take part in nuclear sharing, and nuclear weapons have been placed in the territories of some of them and can at any moment be placed in the territories of others. It is these States who should exercise maximum transparency and report in as great detail as possible their nuclear infrastructure, its use and their plans for the future.

entries.position_summary: They denounce the stationing of U.S. nuclear weapons in Europe as a direct threat and call for their complete withdrawal to U.S. territory and dismantlement of related infrastructure. They warn that their presence provokes compensatory countermeasures.
entries.quote: In the context of the overall growth of threats from the West, the retention of U.S. nuclear weapons in Europe, which are designed to promptly hit a wide range of targets in the Russian territory, is of major concern to us, inciting compensatory countermeasures. These weapons must be completely withdrawn to the U.S. terri- tory and the relevant infrastructure in Europe must be dismantled.

entries.position_summary: The country criticizes U.S. extended deterrence and joint nuclear planning with the Republic of Korea, arguing these arrangements heighten regional tensions and fuel an arms race. It also opposes expanding this model to include Japan.
entries.quote: Washington's steps toward extending such schemes to other parts of the world, where the United States already practices so-called "extended deterrence," also have pronounced negative implications for regional and global security. In particular, the arrangements between the United States and the Republic of Korea on joint "nuclear planning" lead to heightened tensions in the Asia-Pacific region and spur an arms race. We note with concern the official calls to expand this format to include Japan.

entries.position_summary: The country criticizes states engaged in nuclear sharing, forward deployment, and joint nuclear planning for obstructing nuclear disarmament and calls their appeals for accelerated disarmament hypocritical. It implies these policies must be addressed or ended to enable progress toward Article VI commitments.
entries.quote: 3 disarmament. It totally escapes us how anyone can call for accelerated nuclear disarmament without taking into account the political reality, especially if the ones who call are the States that allow forward deployment of foreign nuclear weapons in their territory, provide military infrastructure for the use of such weapons and take part in 'nuclear sharing' and joint nuclear planning. It is these countries' irresponsible policy that has become a major obstacle to the nuclear disarmament.

entries.position_summary: The country condemns U.S.-ROK joint nuclear planning and the visit of a U.S. nuclear-armed ballistic missile submarine to Busan, arguing these actions undermine efforts and UN Security Council resolutions aimed at denuclearizing the Korean Peninsula. It assigns responsibility to Washington and Seoul.
entries.quote: 5 referred to joint nuclear planning. On 18 July, a U.S. nuclear-armed ballistic missile submarine visited the port of Busan. Such actions undermine not only efforts to resolve the situation on the Korean Peninsula, but also the entire system of UN Security Council resolutions aimed at its denuclearization. The responsibility for this falls on Washington and Seoul.

entries.position_summary: The country calls for discussions on providing assurances to non-nuclear-weapon States that are allied with nuclear-weapon States or under bilateral security agreements (the nuclear umbrella). It seeks consideration of security guarantees tailored to NNWS engaged in extended deterrence arrangements.
entries.quote: The main issues to be discussed should include possible assurances to those non-nuclear-weapon States that make part of alliances with nuclear powers or have concluded bilateral security agreements with them (the so-called 'nuclear umbrella').

entries.position_summary: The country condemns NATO nuclear sharing and the forward deployment of U.S. nuclear weapons in Europe as destabilizing, increasing strategic risks, and obstructing progress on nuclear disarmament, especially amid modernization of these capabilities. It calls for the complete withdrawal of U.S. nuclear weapons to U.S. territory and dismantlement of the related infrastructure in Europe.
entries.quote: Particular emphasis should be put on the destabilizing practice of the NATO's so-called "nuclear sharing" involving forward-based U.S. nuclear weapons deployed in Europe, that is, thousands miles away from the U.S., and capable of promptly hitting critical targets in the territory of Russia and its allies. Given the general increase in threats posed by the West and active modernization of the mentioned nuclear capabilities, this practice increases strategic risks and prompts one to take compensating counter-measures. This factor has considerably complicated the dialogue at the NPT forum for decades, and has long been a major obstacle to further steps in the field of nuclear disarmament. We reiterate that the United States' nuclear weapons must be completely withdrawn to their national territory and the relevant infrastructure in Europe must be dismantled.

entries.position_summary: The statement calls for implementing NPT Article VI through the engagement of all nuclear-armed states and active participation by all countries, including non-nuclear-weapon states under nuclear umbrellas. It urges these states to contribute meaningfully to improving the international environment to achieve disarmament objectives.
entries.quote: The process of nuclear disarmament requires the engagement of all States possessing military nuclear capabilities. What is more, for all the elements of the NPT Article VI to be implemented in their entirety, active involvement of all countries will be necessary, including non-nuclear-weapon States, especially those under nuclear umbrella. They should also make a meaningful contribution to improving the international environment and achieving the Treaty's disarmament objectives.

entries.position_summary: The country criticizes the AUKUS arrangement, warning it could enable nuclear-weapon States to build military infrastructure and potentially deploy nuclear weapons on the territory of non-nuclear-weapon States party to the Treaty of Rarotonga. It argues this risk undermines the South Pacific Nuclear-Weapon-Free Zone and validates its prior reservations.
entries.quote: Such clarifying reservations have been wholly justified, as the situation around the United States', United Kingdom's and Australia's AUKUS deal has demonstrated: one cannot exclude the possibility that as a result of this partnership nuclear-weapon States create their military infrastructure and, in the future, nuclear weapons in the territory of non-nuclear-weapon States parties to the Treaty of Rarotonga.

entries.position_summary: The country contends that U.S. extended deterrence measures with the Republic of Korea—highlighted by an SSBN port visit and claims of a “nuclear” alliance—contradict nonproliferation goals and undermine denuclearization of the Korean Peninsula. It argues that Washington, not the DPRK, bears primary responsibility for the lack of progress on denuclearization.
entries.quote: For many years, it has been common to blame the DPRK for violating the NPT and the lack of progress in denuclearization of the Korean Peninsula. In fact, it is the United States that is taking steps incompatible with the objectives of nuclear non-proliferation by drawing the Republic of Korea, a non-nuclear-weapon State within the meaning of the NPT, into the orbit of its nuclear strategy. The President of the Republic of Korea has openly declared that the alliance with the U.S. has "upgraded to a nuclear one." On 18 July, a U.S. nuclear-armed intercontinental ballistic missile submarine visited the port of Busan. Such actions nullify the prospects for resolving the situation on the Korean Peninsula, undermining the goal of its denuclearization, which is backed by relevant UN Security Council resolutions. The responsibility for this falls on Washington.

entries.position_summary: The statement criticizes U.S./NATO nuclear modernization and potential geographic expansion of U.S. nuclear deployments in Europe as escalating strategic risks, prompting Russia and Belarus to take compensatory nuclear measures for deterrence. It asserts that Russian-Belarusian nuclear cooperation complies with international law and the NPT and does not threaten third countries.
entries.quote: Such steps severely aggravate strategic risks and general atmosphere of tension and elicit responses aimed at restoring the balance of forces and maintaining reliable deterrence. In particular, due to a quantum leap in the development of combat capability of the US nuclear weapons deployed in Europe and posing strategic threat, potential expansion of the geographic scope of its deployment, as well as general increase of diverse threats posed by NATO, the Russian Federation and the Republic of Belarus have had to resort to compensating steps in the nuclear field. Nevertheless, Russian-Belarusian cooperation in this sphere strictly complies with the international law and our international obligations, including the provisions of the NPT, and poses no threat to third countries.

entries.position_summary: The country criticizes U.S. extended nuclear deterrence and joint nuclear planning with the ROK (and possible involvement of Japan) as destabilizing and fueling an arms race in the Asia-Pacific. It opposes U.S. deployment and transfer of nuclear-capable delivery systems to allies—especially submarines for Australia—arguing this raises compliance concerns under the NPT and the Treaty of Rarotonga.
entries.quote: Washington's steps to replicate such schemes in other parts of the world where the United States already practices its so-called "extended nuclear deterrence," also have extremely negative implications for regional and global security. In particular, the United States' and Republic of Korea's joint "nuclear planning" activities accompanied by calls to involve Japan in the process, create considerable tension in the Asia-Pacific and spur arms race. These tensions are fuelled by Washington's active steps to deploy its strategic platforms in the region, including nuclear delivery vehicles, and plans to transfer systems that could carry nuclear weapons, to their allies. In particular, the United States intends to transfer to Australia submarines designed, among other things, to carry nuclear cruise missiles that are under development. This poses a number of serious questions in the context of both the NPT and the Treaty of Rarotonga.

entries.position_summary: The country condemns AUKUS, arguing it establishes nuclear-weapons-state infrastructure in a non-nuclear-weapon, NWFZ country, creating the potential for future nuclear weapon deployment. It views this as destabilizing and detrimental to nuclear disarmament efforts in the Asia-Pacific.
entries.quote: Mr. Chair, We disapprove of the AUKUS trilateral partnership. It creates a fundamentally new geopolitical situation in the Asia-Pacific region. Within the partnership, an infrastructure of nuclear-weapon States, which can later be used to deploy nuclear weapons, is being established in a non-nuclear-weapon State that is also a NWFZ country. This produces an additional factor of instability that undermines nuclear disarmament efforts.

entries.position_summary: The country contends that progress toward nuclear disarmament has stalled due to a worsening security environment. It blames NATO’s hostile actions and the Alliance’s maintenance and further development of nuclear-sharing arrangements and its identity as a 'nuclear alliance' for obstructing the conclusion and implementation of new disarmament agreements. It criticizes the elevated role of nuclear weapons in NATO doctrine as destabilizing.
entries.quote: We have to state that at the moment, the processes intended to result in a nuclear- weapon-free world together with general and complete disarmament, have currently lost most of their momentum. Current international security and strategic stability situation objectively prevents any progress on this track. In our national and joint statements we have repeatedly pointed at the factors that have had severe negative influence on politico- military and strategic environment, creating major obstacles to both reaching and effectively implementing new agreements on disarmament initiatives. The factors with most destabilizing effect include NATO countries' hostile actions posing risk of a direct military confrontation between the nuclear powers, the preservation and further development of the arrangements and capabilities for the so-called 'nuclear sharing' by the North Atlantic Alliance, which has proclaimed itself to be a 'nuclear alliance', the

entries.position_summary: The statement argues that disarmament progress requires the West to stop policies perceived as undermining Russia’s security and for all nuclear-armed states to join the disarmament process. It highlights NATO’s role as a “nuclear alliance,” the combined U.S.-UK-French capabilities, and criticizes the UK’s expansion of its arsenal. The thrust is that NATO’s posture and allied arsenals impede disarmament and must be addressed in future arms control.
entries.quote: Thus, further progress on the nuclear disarmament track will require the West to abandon its destructive policy of undermining Russia's security. At the same time, it will be necessary for all the States with military nuclear capabilities to join the dis- armament process. First of all, in this context we have to take into account the com- bined arsenal of the United States, United Kingdom and France, which are NATO al- lies and are capable of jointly using nuclear forces against our country. This factor takes on special significance given the fact that the North Atlantic bloc, positioning itself as a "nuclear alliance," is being increasingly involved in the confrontation with Russia and declares its intention to inflict a "strategic defeat" on Russia. Moreover, the nuclear arsenals of London and Paris are not only unlimited, but in the case of the United Kingdom, they are being significantly increased.

entries.position_summary: The country argues NATO has become a nuclear bloc whose shared strategy relies on nuclear weapons, rendering UK and French arsenals as direct threats alongside the United States. It criticizes the United Kingdom’s announced buildup of its nuclear arsenal as running counter to international expectations to reduce nuclear weapons and undermining disarmament obligations.
entries.quote: At the same time, the military and political situation in the world continues to deteriorate rapidly. The accumulated conflict potential between nuclear powers, which was not curbed in time, has already led to an acute crisis in Euro-Atlantic and threatens to escalate into a military confrontation in Asia-Pacific. Having violated the principle of equal and indivisible security, the NATO bloc has staked on expansion and assertive involvement of more and more members. Besides, NATO has officially become a nuclear bloc, which means that all its States pursue shared strategic goals, which are ensured, inter alia, by nuclear weapons. In this case, nuclear weapons of the United Kingdom and France become as much of a threat to Russia as nuclear weapons of the United States. Moreover, against the backdrop of the international community's demand to reduce nuclear weapons, London has announced a build-up of its nuclear arsenal.

entries.position_summary: The statement condemns NATO as an illegitimate nuclear bloc whose aggressive nuclear posture and sharing arrangements push the world toward nuclear war, urging a collective international response. It portrays NATO’s reliance on nuclear weapons in its security doctrine as destabilizing and a threat to global security.
entries.quote: 3 obviously had a hand in this. An aggressive, bloodthirsty, illegitimate nuclear military bloc, NATO has put the world on the brink of a global nuclear war with the only purpose of checking Russia's reaction to its aggressive behaviour. Such steps on the part of NATO should receive proper assessment and response of all countries interested in ensuring their security.
